step.title: Step 16
step.actionDisplay: RIGHT -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> RIGHT -> RESET

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 7 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
The game is over.
Current state: step 130, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 129 actions; a strong score needs about 54 or fewer. You are 2.4x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 129 actions returned the board to a state already seen 129 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 129 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

This happened because I tried to find 'cddd7d2c' in cycle 1, but cycle 1 is ['c66a83c4', '4d73d897', 'f374da18', '9f50b466', '886b69c2', '3ad80649', 'fb80c9b2'], which doesn't contain 'cddd7d2c'. So my hypothesis that all PK positions can be set to 'cddd7d2c' is wrong - position 1 cannot be set to 'cddd7d2c'.
But more importantly, the game is over. The last_action_result shows 'game_over': True and 'state': 'GAME_OVER'. This means I cannot continue playing.
Wait, but the system message says "Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT." and "You are still on the same level." So maybe the game is not actually over? Let me check the last_action_result again.
Looking at the output: "state': 'GAME_OVER'" and "game_over': True". So the game IS over.
But the system message says "You are still on the same level." This is confusing. Maybe "GAME_OVER" means the level failed (not completed), and I need to restart or move to the next level.
Actually, looking more carefully, the system message says "The game is over." which suggests the entire game is over, not just the level. But the valid actions are still UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, which suggests I can still take actions.
Let me try to take an action and see what happens. If the game is truly over, the action might not change anything or might trigger a restart.
Actually, I think the issue is that the game timed out or reached a maximum number of actions. The 'run_elapsed_seconds' was 4243 seconds (about 70 minutes), and 'time_remaining_seconds' is 3676 seconds (about 61 minutes). So there's still time remaining, but the game is in a GAME_OVER state.
Wait, I just noticed something. The PK panel is now ['a2774f59', 'c66a83c4', '5fcaa599', '8b720570', 'cddd7d2c'], which is the initial state! This means the game reset the PK panel to the initial state when the game ended.
But the system message says "NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 129 actions returned the board to a state already seen 129 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 129 actions." This confirms that the PK panel is back to the initial state.
So the game is over, and I need to figure out what the correct target was. Let me think about this more carefully.

Actually, looking at the system message again: "Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT." This suggests I can still take actions. Let me try to take an action and see if the game responds.
But the last_action_result shows 'game_over': True. So maybe the game is truly over and I can't make any more progress.

Row 1: S(2), P(2)=fb80c9b2, S(3), P(3)=f4469c24

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game appears to be over, but let me verify and try one more hypothesis
# The PK is back to initial state: ['a2774f59', 'c66a83c4', '5fcaa599', '8b720570', 'cddd7d2c']
# Let me check if I can still take actions

print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', False)}")
print(f"State: {last_action_result.get('state', 'unknown')}")

# Try a simple action to see if game responds
result = action(['UP'])
print(f"After UP: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
pink_panel = [n for n in nodes if n['id'] == 48][0]
pk_after = [nodes[c]['hash'][:8] for c in pink_panel['children']]
print(f"PK after UP: {pk_after}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
